data.title: International Paper announces facility alignment in Rio Grande Valley
data.description:
• International Paper (NYSE:IP) on Thursday announced the consolidation of its operations in the Rio Grande Valley.
  • As part of its strategic growth initiative, the company will make strategic investments to convert the current Edinburg, Tex. sheet plant into a warehouse, invest in its current facility in McAllen, Tex. to increase capabilities and shift its current Reynosa, Mexico operations to a new, more modern and capable facility that is currently under construction in Reynosa.
  • The company will close its box plant and sheet plant in Edinburg, Tex.
  • "The company's goal is to minimize the impact to affected employees through natural attrition, retirements and open positions at other IP facilities, including open positions at our McAllen, Tex. facility. The company will assist employees and customers through this transition," it said.
  • IP -1.35% premarket to $44.31.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: HubSpot dips despite 'solid' Q1 amid 'pressure on deal metrics'
data.description:
Shares of HubSpot (NYSE:HUBS) fell about 4% premarket on Friday after first-quarter results and outlook, which largely drew positive views from analysts.

BofA Securities kept its Buy rating on HubSpot and raised the price objective on the stock to $740 from 700.

"HubSpot delivered a solid Q1 and outlook, though offset by slight pressure on deal metrics," said analysts led by Brad Sills.

On the positives, the analysts noted that first quarter revenue and billings growth of 18% constant currency exceeded their estimate of 15%, driven by seat expansion from core seat additions to include non-sales users (e.g., ops/admins). Net revenue retention, or NRR, of 102% (down from 104% in Q4) was not encouraging, though the downtick is largely expected given fourth quarter upgrade seasonality, according to the analysts.

Finally, average selling price, or ASP, growth of -4% year-over-year (down from -1% in the fourth quarter) was due to a larger-than-expected customer net additions. Commentary suggests ASP growth will remain flat for the next couple of quarters, the analysts added.

Morgan Stanley maintained its Overweight rating on HubSpot and increased the price target on the stock to $752 from $659.

Analyst Elizabeth Porter and her team said HubSpot's solid first quarter print comes on the back of strong recent out-performance and may leave investors looking for more near-term. Multi-hub momentum, upmarket success, and growing AI traction builds confidence in the durability of growth, according to the analysts.

Related stock: Salesforce (CRM) is up about 1% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Chip stocks in focus as Onto Innovation, Wolfspeed plunge after earnings
data.description:
Semiconductor stocks were in focus on Friday after a handful of companies in the sector reported quarterly results, including Onto Innovation (NYSE:ONTO) and Wolfspeed (NYSE:WOLF).

Onto Innovation shares tumbled nearly 14% in premarket trading after the company reported better-than-expected first-quarter results, but analysts expressed concern about its guidance and the impact of tariffs. Investment firm Stifel downgraded Onto to Hold from Buy and cut its price target to $117 following the results. Benchmark also lowered its price target to $190 from $230 after the results.

Wolfspeed shares crashed nearly 20% after it reported third-quarter results and saw a pair of downgrades from Citi and J.P. Morgan. Citi cut its rating to Sell and lowered its price target to $3, while J.P. Morgan cut its rating to Underweight from Neutral. Piper Sandler was also negative on the Durham, N.C.-based company and lowered its price target to $6 from $10.

Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) shares fell 2.4% after the company issued a weaker-than-expected forecast for the second-quarter that overshadowed its first-quarter results.

Synaptics (SYNA) shares rose 0.3% in premarket trading after its third-quarter results topped estimates and issued a forecast for the coming quarter that was largely in-line with expectations. 

Elsewhere in the sector, Alpha & Omega (AOSL) had its price target cut at Benchmark to $32 from $42, while MKS Instruments had a couple of price target cuts (Mizuho and Deutsche Bank) despite reporting better-than-expected results earlier this week.

data.title: SA Charts: How did major U.S. airlines stack against each other in Q1?
data.description:
All major airlines saw a sequential fall in PRASM in the first quarter. United Airlines saw the biggest decline among the bunch. Meanwhile, Southwest has continued to strengthen its PRASM on a Y/Y basis over the past three quarters. Other carriers saw meager changes in their PRASM on a year-on-year basis.

 

.

TRASM fell for all major airlines in the first quarter. Here, too, United Airlines saw the most sequential decline, with American Airlines coming second. On a year-on-year basis, there were few TRASM improvements. Delta was the only airline to see TRASM dip compared to last year.

 

 

Unit cost movement was not similar for all the major carriers. American and Southwest saw a Y/Y increase in CASM, while Delta and United saw dipping unit costs compared to last year. On a sequential basis, United was the only carrier to see CASM tick lower.

data.title: BP rises as rivals said to weigh possible takeover - FT
data.description:
BP (NYSE:BP) +3.1% pre-market Friday following a Financial Times report that several rival oil producers have "run the numbers" in weighing the opportunity to buy the struggling company at a steep discount.

Shell (SHEL), Chevron (CVX), Exxon Mobil (XOM), TotalEnergies (TTE) and Abu Dhabi's Adnoc have been analyzing a potential deal, while oil trader Vitol could be interested in elements of the business, according to the report.

Takeover talk started earlier this week from a Bloomberg report that Shell (SHEL) was studying the possibility of a BP (NYSE:BP) purchase.

Ongoing underperformance by BP (BP) shares - the stock has dropped 27% over 12 months - "makes it open to a takeover," a source close to activist investor Elliott Management, which holds a stake of just over 5% in the company, told FT.

BP's (BP) oil and gas assets alone are worth $82B, according to UBS analyst Joshua Stone - above the current $75B market value - but the company is weighed down by $77Bn of debt and long-term liabilities, including those stemming from the 2010 Deepwater Horizon oil spill.

data.title: 1-800-Flowers.com shares wilt on bleak Q1 results; CEO steps down
data.description:
Shares of 1-800-Flowers.com (NASDAQ:FLWS) are limping through Friday’s premarket trading and expected to open at the lowest level in more than a decade as gloomy first quarter results drove shares more than 30% lower ahead of the open.

“While we are deeply disappointed by the quarterly results, we are steadfast in our commitment to turning this underperformance around," said Jim McCann the current CEO of 1-800-Flowers.com.

Reflecting on the disappointing results and need for innovative ideas and new leadership for the company, CEO McCann made the decision to step down from the company he founded nearly five decades ago, passing the torch to former Home Depot (HD) executive Adolfo Villagomez.

“In this case, the best thing I can do is fire myself and hire someone who’s much more skilled than I am in the areas that are important to the company going forward,” McCann said in an interview with The Wall Street Journal.

During the critical holiday season, issues tied to order fulfillment with Harry & David products, inaccurate orders, and damaged products reportedly cost the company as much as $20M in lost sales and refunds, reverberating through the first quarter in diminished demand and resulting 12.6% decline in revenue to $331.5M, $33M less than Wall Street expected. This contributed to an adjusted loss of $0.71 per share, significantly worse than a loss of $0.28 per share in the same quarter last year and below expectations by $0.37. Adjusted EBITDA loss widened to $34.9M, versus a loss of $18.0M last year.

The $4.6M cost to replace and refund orders during the Q4 technical snafu, coupled with a highly promotional sales environment, weighed on the company’s gross profit margin, which declined by 350 basis points to 33.1%.

By category, sales in the company’s Consumer Floral & Gifts segment were down 11.4% and fell 18.2% for Gourmet Foods and Gift Baskets. BloomNet, a network of florists used to fulfill orders and offer various business solutions to florists, realized a 4.5% improvement in sales.

The latest quarterly results, along with the “rapidly evolving macroeconomic landscape and uncertainties that continue to shape the near-term outlook,” led 1-800-Flowers.com (NASDAQ:FLWS) to withdraw its near-term guidance.
